title: The Odyssey: Rendered into English prose for the use of those who cannot read the original
author: Homer
summary: The Odyssey recounts Ulysses’ long return from Troy to his home at Ithaca. Held by Calypso and buffeted by gods, especially Poseidon, he wanders widely, suffers many woes, and loses his companions when they offend the gods. Athena and other immortals intercede; Hermes and the Phaeacians aid his voyage. Parallelly, at home Penelope and Telemachus confront suitors and wait for news. The poem interweaves Ulysses’ narrated adventures, divine councils, and human vengeance (Aegisthus slain by Orestes). Butler’s prose rendering clarifies episodes, classical references, and textual relations with the Iliad while preserving epic scope and vivid narrative voice rich in digression.

characters.name: Odysseus (Ulysses)
characters.personality: resourceful, cunning, suffering hero; longing for home
characters.lifespan: survives long wanderings, held by Calypso, ultimately returns to Ithaca

characters.name: Penelope
characters.lifespan: awaits Odysseus at home, fends off suitors

characters.name: Telemachus
characters.personality: evolving, dutiful son seeking news of his father
characters.lifespan: travels to seek information (Pylos episode), helps restore order at home

characters.name: Athene (Athena)
characters.personality: compassionate, strategic divine patron of Odysseus
characters.lifespan: immortal; intercedes on Odysseus's behalf among the gods

characters.name: Zeus
characters.lifespan: immortal; presides over divine councils concerning Odysseus

characters.name: Calypso
characters.lifespan: keeps Odysseus on her isle until the gods order his release

characters.name: Nausicaa
characters.personality: courteous, hospitable; identified by Butler with the poet in preface
characters.lifespan: helps Odysseus when he reaches the Phaeacians (Phaeacian episode)

characters.name: Hermes
characters.lifespan: delivers divine commands (warns Aegisthus; aids Odysseus indirectly)

characters.name: Aegisthus
characters.lifespan: takes Agamemnon's wife and murders Agamemnon; is later slain by Orestes (explicitly mentioned)

characters.name: Orestes
characters.lifespan: slays Aegisthus to avenge Agamemnon (explicitly mentioned)

characters.name: Helios (Hyperion)
characters.lifespan: withdraws crew's day of return after they slaughter his cattle (mentioned)

characters.name: Agamemnon
characters.lifespan: returned from Troy and was murdered by Aegisthus (recounted)

characters.name: Phaeacians
characters.lifespan: aid Odysseus in returning home (Phaeacian episode referenced)

characters.name: Argos
characters.lifespan: referenced in relation to Hermes and slayer imagery (mentioned in translation)
